label: Microsoft OneDrive
description: Arcade.dev LLM tools for Microsoft OneDrive

tools.name: CopyItem
tools.description:
Copy a file or folder and wait for the copy to finish, returning the new item.

Microsoft Graph performs copies asynchronously; this tool polls the operation to
completion within a bounded budget so the caller receives the finished item directly.
A stale or mistyped source id returns a structured not_found envelope (status
"not_found", retryable false) rather than a fatal error, so the caller can branch on
the field.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the item to copy.

parameters.name: destination_folder_id
parameters.description: Optional destination folder ID. If omitted, the item is copied to the same folder.

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.description: Optional new name for the copied item.

parameters.name: conflict_behavior
parameters.description: How to resolve a name that already exists in the destination folder. Defaults to FAIL (report the conflict without copying).

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateFolder
tools.description: Create a new folder in OneDrive.

parameters.name: folder_name
parameters.description: The name of the new folder.

parameters.name: parent_folder_id
parameters.description: Optional parent folder ID. If omitted, creates in the root.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: CreateShareLink
tools.description:
Create a share link for a OneDrive item.

Set link_type to EDIT to let recipients change the item; use scope ORGANIZATION to keep
the link inside the tenant instead of anyone-with-the-link.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the file or folder to share.

parameters.name: link_type
parameters.description: Whether the link grants read-only (view) or read-write (edit) access. Defaults to VIEW.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Who can use the link: anyone with the link (anonymous) or only people inside the organization (organization). Defaults to ANONYMOUS.

parameters.name: expiration_date
parameters.description: Date the link stops working (YYYY-MM-DD). Omit for a link that never expires.

parameters.name: password
parameters.description: Password required to open the link. Omit for no password. Only honored on anonymous links.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: DeleteItem
tools.description:
Delete one or more files or folders from OneDrive in a single call.

An item open for editing elsewhere is reported as locked and retryable rather than
failing the whole batch; every requested id is attempted.

parameters.name: item_ids
parameters.description: IDs of the files or folders to delete. Pass one id or many; each is attempted independently so one locked item never aborts the rest.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: DownloadFile
tools.description:
Download a OneDrive file's content as base64, paging large files by byte offset.

Files at or under the per-call cap return fully in one call; for larger files, re-call
with offset set to the returned next_offset until is_final is true. A stale or mistyped
id returns a structured not_found envelope (status "not_found", retryable false) rather
than a fatal error, so the caller can branch on the field.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the file to download.

parameters.name: drive_id
parameters.description: The drive containing the file. Omit to use the current user's OneDrive.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-indexed byte offset of the page to fetch. Defaults to 0 (start of file).

parameters.name: max_bytes
parameters.description: Maximum raw bytes to return on this call (1 to 5242880). Defaults to 16384 (16 KiB), sized so the base64 page fits an agent's tool-result budget; raise it to fetch a larger window per call, lower it for a tighter budget.

output.description: A page of the file's content as base64.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: FindDuplicateFiles
tools.description:
Find files that are byte-identical copies of each other across folders in one call.

Groups examined files by content fingerprint and returns only the fingerprints with two
or more copies, each group flagging the copy to keep and the rest as deletion candidates,
so a de-dup cleanup can act without listing and grouping by hash manually.
The scan walks the drive directly rather than the search index, so a file copied moments
ago is seen immediately. Supply folder_id to scope the scan to a subtree (much more likely to
be complete, though still bounded by limit and an internal walk cap); keywords further
restrict to files whose name contains the term.
When truncated is true, truncated_warning explains what was missed and how to refocus.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Case-insensitive substring matched against file names; only files whose name contains it are examined, so a focused term finds copies of a specific file. Leave empty to scan every file regardless of name (a whole-drive de-dup audit), bounded by the same scan cap. Can be combined with folder_id to restrict to files matching this term within a specific subtree — the two filters are applied simultaneously. The scan walks the drive directly rather than the search index, so files written moments ago are included.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum files to examine in one scan (1 to 200). Defaults to 200. Lower it to speed up a focused scan; the response flags when this cap truncated the scan.

parameters.name: folder_id
parameters.description: Scope the scan to a specific folder's subtree. When provided, only files within that folder and its subfolders are examined, which makes a complete result far more likely for a focused subtree. The scan is still bounded by limit and an internal walk cap, so a very large subtree can still be truncated — check the truncated flag. Omit to scan from the drive root.

parameters.name: keep_by_name_contains
parameters.description: Override the default newest-copy keep heuristic: within each duplicate group, the copy whose name contains this string (case-insensitive) is flagged as keep_item_id. If no copy in a group matches, the newest copy is kept. Leave empty to always keep the newest copy.

output.description: Groups of byte-identical files found among the matches.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetCopyStatus
tools.description: Check status of an async copy operation using the token returned by copy_item.

parameters.name: operation_url
parameters.description: The opaque operation token returned by copy_item. Pass it exactly as received.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetItem
tools.description:
Resolve a single OneDrive file or folder's metadata directly from its id.

A stale or mistyped id returns a structured not_found envelope (status "not_found",
retryable false) rather than a fatal error, so the caller can branch on the field.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the file or folder to resolve.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetMyDrive
tools.description: Get metadata about the user's OneDrive (id, name, quota, owner).

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: GetSharedWithMe
tools.description: List files shared with the current user.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return in this page (1 to 50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's next_token to fetch the next page. Pass it back unchanged; omit for the first page.

output.description: The items shared with the current user.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListFolderItems
tools.description: List files and folders in a OneDrive folder. Lists root if folder_id is omitted.

parameters.name: folder_id
parameters.description: The ID of the folder to list items from. If not provided, lists items from the root.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return in this page (1 to 50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's next_token to fetch the next page. Pass it back unchanged; omit for the first page.

output.description: The items from the specified folder or root.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: ListItemPermissions
tools.description:
List who can currently access a OneDrive item: every share link and direct grant on it.

Use this to answer "who can see this?" and to find the permission_id needed to revoke a
grant. A stale or mistyped id returns a structured not_found envelope (status "not_found",
retryable false) rather than a fatal error, so the caller can branch on the field. When
has_more is true, pass next_token back unchanged to fetch the remaining grants.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the file or folder whose sharing grants to list.

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's next_token to fetch the next page of grants. Pass it back unchanged; omit for the first page.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: MoveItem
tools.description:
Move and/or rename one or more files or folders in OneDrive in a single call.

Provide new_parent_id to relocate items, new_name (single item only) to rename in place,
or both. At least one must be supplied. An item open for editing elsewhere is reported as
locked and retryable rather than failing the whole batch.

parameters.name: item_ids
parameters.description: IDs of the items to move and/or rename. Pass one id or many; every listed item is moved into the same new_parent_id.

parameters.name: new_parent_id
parameters.description: Destination folder ID to move every listed item into. Omit to leave items in their current folder (rename only).

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.description: New name for the item, including its extension. Only valid when exactly one id is given; omit to leave names unchanged.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: RevokeItemPermission
tools.description:
Revoke one or all anonymous sharing grants on a OneDrive item.

Single-grant mode (revoke_all_anonymous=false): removes the grant identified by
permission_id. An unknown item or permission id returns a structured not_found envelope
rather than a fatal error. To revoke an inherited grant, target the parent folder instead.

Bulk mode (revoke_all_anonymous=true): removes every anonymous (anyone-with-the-link) grant
on the item in one call. revoked_count reports how many were removed; 0 means none existed.

parameters.name: item_id
parameters.description: The ID of the file or folder the permission is on.

parameters.name: permission_id
parameters.description: The ID of the sharing grant to revoke. Required unless revoke_all_anonymous is true.

parameters.name: revoke_all_anonymous
parameters.description: When true, revokes every anonymous sharing link on the item in one call. A common job is removing all open-web links from a file; this makes that a single call instead of one revoke per link — list the item's permissions first to see what will be removed. When true, permission_id is ignored. Defaults to false (single-grant mode, permission_id required).

output.description: Outcome of revoking the sharing grant(s).

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: SearchItems
tools.description:
Search for files and folders in the user's OneDrive.

It may take a few seconds to minutes for the search index to update with newly created items.

parameters.name: keywords
parameters.description: Keywords to search for items in OneDrive.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The number of items to return in this page (1 to 50). Defaults to 25.

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's next_token to fetch the next page. Pass it back unchanged; omit for the first page.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

tools.name: WhoAmI
tools.description: Identify the current user and confirm OneDrive access for orientation.

output.description: The current user's identity and OneDrive environment.

codeExample.tabLabel: Call the Tool with User Authorization

summary:
Microsoft OneDrive toolkit for Arcade provides LLM-ready tools for managing files, folders, permissions, and sharing in a user's OneDrive via Microsoft Graph.

## Capabilities

- **File & folder operations:** Create folders, copy (with async polling to completion), move/rename (single or batch), delete (single or batch), and download files (with byte-offset paging for large files).
- **Browse & search:** List folder contents, list files shared with the current user, search the drive index, and retrieve metadata for a single item or the drive itself.
- **Permissions & sharing:** Create share links (view/edit, user/organization scope), list all grants on an item (links and direct grants), and revoke a single grant or all anonymous links in bulk.
- **Duplicate detection:** Scan a drive or subtree for byte-identical files, grouped by content fingerprint, with deletion candidates flagged — no manual hashing required.
- **Resilient error handling:** Stale or missing item IDs return structured `not_found` envelopes (with a `retryable` flag) instead of fatal errors; locked items in batch operations are reported individually without aborting the batch.
- **Identity & orientation:** Confirm the authenticated user's identity and validate OneDrive access before performing operations.

## OAuth

This toolkit authenticates via OAuth 2.0 using **Microsoft** as the provider. See the [Arcade Microsoft auth provider docs](https://docs.arcade.dev/en/references/auth-providers/microsoft) for setup details.
